name: Pop Culture
description: From viral memes to celebrity moments, how well do you know pop culture?

questions.text: Which platform launched the viral 'Ice Bucket Challenge' in 2014?
questions.explanation: The ALS Ice Bucket Challenge went viral primarily on Facebook in 2014, raising over $115 million for ALS research.

questions.text: What year did Instagram launch?
questions.explanation: Instagram launched in October 2010 for iOS and was acquired by Facebook in 2012.

questions.text: Which artist released the album 'Lemonade' in 2016?
questions.explanation: Beyoncé released 'Lemonade' as a visual album in April 2016 to critical acclaim.

questions.text: What was the name of the viral 2012 song by PSY that popularized the 'horse dance'?
questions.explanation: 'Gangnam Style' by PSY became the first YouTube video to reach 1 billion views in 2012.

questions.text: Which streaming service produced 'Stranger Things'?
questions.explanation: Stranger Things is an original Netflix series that premiered in July 2016.

questions.text: What is the name of the coffee chain known for writing (often misspelling) customers' names on cups?
questions.explanation: Starbucks became famous for writing customers' names on cups, often with creative misspellings that people share on social media.

questions.text: Which social media platform uses the term 'tweet' for posts?
questions.explanation: Twitter, now rebranded as X, uses the term 'tweet' for posts made on its platform.

questions.text: What animal is the logo of Twitter (now X) historically associated with?
questions.explanation: Twitter's iconic blue bird logo was named Larry the Bird, after NBA star Larry Bird.

questions.text: Which Disney animated film features the song 'Let It Go'?
questions.explanation: 'Let It Go' is a song from Disney's Frozen (2013), performed by Idina Menzel as Queen Elsa.

questions.text: What does 'FOMO' stand for?
questions.explanation: FOMO stands for 'Fear of Missing Out', the anxiety of missing exciting events or experiences.

questions.text: Which game became a cultural phenomenon when it launched in summer 2016 with AR gameplay?
questions.explanation: Pokémon GO was released in July 2016 and became a global phenomenon with its augmented reality gameplay.

questions.text: What is the name of Elon Musk's space exploration company?
questions.explanation: SpaceX was founded by Elon Musk in 2002 with the goal of reducing space transportation costs.

questions.text: Which TV show featured the catchphrase 'Winter is Coming'?
questions.explanation: 'Winter is Coming' is the motto of House Stark in HBO's Game of Thrones, based on George R.R. Martin's novels.

questions.text: What does 'TBT' stand for on social media?
questions.explanation: TBT stands for Throwback Thursday, a social media trend where users post old photos on Thursdays.

questions.text: Which social media app is known for disappearing messages and Stories?
questions.explanation: Snapchat pioneered disappearing messages and Stories, features that were later adopted by other platforms.

questions.text: Who played Iron Man in the Marvel Cinematic Universe?
questions.explanation: Robert Downey Jr. played Tony Stark / Iron Man in the MCU, starting with Iron Man (2008).

questions.text: Which fast food chain is associated with the slogan 'I'm Lovin' It'?
questions.explanation: McDonald's introduced 'I'm Lovin' It' as its global slogan in 2003, accompanied by a jingle featuring Justin Timberlake.

questions.text: The viral 'distracted boyfriend' meme, widely used online, originated as what?
options: A scene from a romantic comedy
A screenshot from a music video
An advertisement for a dating app
questions.explanation: The distracted boyfriend meme originated from a stock photo taken by Spanish photographer Antonio Guillem in 2015.

questions.text: Which year did TikTok (formerly Musical.ly) launch under the TikTok brand internationally?
questions.explanation: TikTok launched internationally in 2018 after ByteDance merged Musical.ly with TikTok.

questions.text: What is the name of the fictional coffee shop where friends hung out in the TV show Friends?
questions.explanation: Central Perk was the iconic coffee shop in Friends where the main characters regularly gathered.

questions.text: Which company owns Instagram, WhatsApp, and Facebook?
questions.explanation: Meta Platforms (formerly Facebook Inc.) owns Instagram, WhatsApp, and Facebook.

questions.text: The phrase 'OK Boomer' became a viral internet trend around what year?
questions.explanation: 'OK Boomer' went viral in late 2019, used by younger generations to dismiss Baby Boomer attitudes.

questions.text: Which video game studio created Fortnite?
questions.explanation: Fortnite was created by Epic Games and launched in 2017, becoming one of the most popular battle royale games.

questions.text: What does the acronym 'GOAT' mean in pop culture?
questions.explanation: GOAT stands for Greatest Of All Time, commonly used to describe elite athletes and celebrities.

questions.text: Which social media platform introduced the concept of 'retweet'?
questions.explanation: Twitter introduced the retweet feature, allowing users to share others' tweets to their followers.

questions.text: What was the name of the first video ever uploaded to YouTube?
questions.explanation: 'Me at the zoo' was uploaded on April 23, 2005 by YouTube co-founder Jawed Karim and is the first video ever posted.

questions.text: Which pop star is known by the nickname 'Queen Bey'?
questions.explanation: Beyoncé Knowles-Carter is widely known as 'Queen Bey', a portmanteau of her name and 'queen'.

questions.text: What does 'IRL' stand for in internet slang?
questions.explanation: IRL stands for 'In Real Life', distinguishing physical interactions from online ones.

questions.text: The viral 'Harlem Shake' videos became popular in early what year?
questions.explanation: The Harlem Shake meme videos went viral in February 2013, featuring people dancing to Baauer's song.

questions.text: Which company created the iPhone?
questions.explanation: Apple Inc. introduced the first iPhone in 2007 under Steve Jobs, revolutionizing the smartphone industry.

questions.text: Which celebrity couple was nicknamed 'Brangelina'?
questions.explanation: Brangelina was the celebrity portmanteau name for Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie during their relationship.

questions.text: What is the name of the viral 2020 game where players discuss who among them is 'among us' (an impostor)?
questions.explanation: Among Us (2018) went massively viral in 2020, with players working to identify impostors in their crew.

questions.text: Who is the founder of Amazon?
questions.explanation: Jeff Bezos founded Amazon in 1994 as an online bookstore before expanding it into a global e-commerce giant.

questions.text: What is the name of the virtual currency used in Fortnite?
questions.explanation: V-Bucks (Vindertech Bucks) is the in-game currency used in Fortnite to purchase cosmetic items.

questions.text: Which TV show introduced the term 'on fleek', later popularized on social media?
options: It was coined in a Vine video, not a TV show
questions.explanation: 'On fleek' was coined by Peaches Monroee in a 2014 Vine video describing her eyebrows, then went viral.

questions.text: What is the 'Streisand Effect' in internet culture?
options: When a celebrity's music goes viral after a controversy
When attempting to suppress information causes it to spread more widely
When a celebrity's social media post accidentally trends
When news stories about celebrities overshadow real news
questions.explanation: The Streisand Effect occurs when trying to suppress information inadvertently draws more attention to it, named after Barbara Streisand's 2003 attempt to suppress photos of her home.

questions.text: What was the original purpose of Reddit's 'upvote' and 'downvote' system, according to its founders?
options: To rank content by popularity for personalized feeds
To surface the most interesting content to the top
To replace a traditional 5-star rating system
To create a democratic way to moderate spam
questions.explanation: Reddit's vote system was designed to collectively surface the most interesting or valuable content within a community to the top of the feed.

questions.text: The 'Overton window' concept, sometimes discussed in pop culture, refers to what?
options: The range of policies acceptable to the public at a given time
The algorithmic filter bubble on social media
The window of time a meme remains culturally relevant
A TV production technique for framing celebrity interviews
questions.explanation: The Overton window describes the range of policies or ideas the public will accept; it was developed by Joseph Overton and has entered wider cultural discussion.

questions.text: Which internet subculture coined the term 'parasocial relationship' to describe fan-celebrity bonds?
options: The term existed in social psychology since the 1950s before internet use
questions.explanation: Parasocial relationship was coined by Horton and Wohl in a 1956 academic paper, long before internet culture adopted and popularized the term.

questions.text: In 2015, an image of a dress went viral because people disagreed on its colors. What were the two contested color interpretations?
options: Red and green OR orange and blue
White and gold OR blue and black
Pink and white OR purple and grey
Yellow and silver OR green and brown
questions.explanation: #TheDress debate in 2015 saw millions argue whether the dress was white and gold or blue and black, driven by differences in color perception and lighting interpretation.

questions.text: What was the name of the NFT artwork by Beeple that sold for $69 million at Christie's in 2021?
questions.explanation: Beeple's 'Everydays: The First 5000 Days' sold for $69.3 million at Christie's in March 2021, marking a watershed moment for NFTs.

questions.text: Which social media concept describes the tendency for algorithms to show users content that reinforces existing beliefs?
questions.explanation: The 'filter bubble', coined by Eli Pariser, describes how personalization algorithms show users content matching their views, limiting exposure to differing perspectives.

questions.text: What was the original name of the company that became Snapchat?
questions.explanation: Snapchat was originally called Picaboo when it launched in 2011 before being rebranded to Snapchat.

questions.text: The 'lonelygirl15' YouTube channel, one of the first viral web series, was later revealed to be what?
options: A documentary project by a film school
A scripted fictional series produced by filmmakers
A marketing campaign for a horror film
An experiment conducted by a university psychology department
questions.explanation: Lonelygirl15 (2006) was revealed to be a scripted web series produced by filmmakers, one of the internet's first major staged 'reality' controversies.

questions.text: Which term describes the phenomenon where companies use minor eco-friendly gestures to appear environmentally responsible without significant change?
questions.explanation: Greenwashing refers to companies misleadingly marketing themselves as environmentally friendly with superficial actions rather than substantive change.

questions.text: The viral 2017 Pepsi ad featuring Kendall Jenner was criticized for trivializing what?
options: Commercial use of classic protest music
questions.explanation: The Pepsi ad was widely condemned for appearing to trivialize Black Lives Matter protests by depicting a protest resolved by Jenner handing a Pepsi to police.

questions.text: What internet phenomenon describes the cycle of a joke becoming so widely repeated it loses humor, then becomes funny again ironically?
questions.explanation: Post-irony describes the layered cycle where a joke becomes earnest again after passing through ironic overuse, common in internet humor culture.

questions.text: The concept of 'stan' culture comes from which Eminem song?
questions.explanation: 'Stan' (2000) by Eminem tells the story of an obsessive fan named Stan; the word is now widely used to describe devoted fans.

questions.text: Which fashion brand popularized the term 'drop culture', releasing limited products unannounced?
questions.explanation: Supreme is credited with pioneering drop culture, releasing limited quantities of products with little notice, creating frenzied demand.

questions.text: What was the name of the disastrous 2017 music festival promoted heavily on social media that turned out to be a fraud?
options: Both B and C are correct names for the same event
questions.explanation: Fyre Festival (also called Fyre Fest) was the fraudulent 2017 luxury music festival in the Bahamas organized by Billy McFarland, documented in multiple Netflix and Hulu films.
